div.head: 283. Editorial Note
div.p: President Eisenhower, accompanied by Prime Minister Macmillan, left the Mid-Ocean Club at 10:10 a.m. on March 24 and drove to Kindley Air Force Base. The President left Bermuda shortly after 10:30. (Eisenhower Library, President’s Appointment Book) Prime Minister Macmillan remained in Bermuda another 3 days. On March 25 and 26, he had conversations with Canadian Prime Minister Louis St. Laurent and Foreign Minister Lester B. Pearson. (Harold Macmillan, Riding the Storm, page 258)
